item.title: Massachusetts flips the switch on Vineyard Wind contracts
item.description: Power purchase agreements signed in 2018 finally activated for 'forever first' project mired in litigation

item.title: Battery giants are signing deals – but the latest energy storage tech still has to prove its business case
item.description: Chinese battery giants are increasingly adding sodium-ion cells to their stack – but what can they actually deliver?

item.title: How Europe's biggest blackout helped turn role of renewables on its head
item.description: Some people blamed Spain's high renewables penetration for last year's blackout, but regulatory changes will now allow wind and solar to help stabilise voltage. Industry leaders say more can be done

item.title: Orsted chief: 'We can't keep building gold-plated offshore wind projects'
item.description: Standardisation crucial to driving down cost of renewable energy from offshore wind farms, says executive at Danish developer

item.title: CIP: We stayed out of ‘stupid auctions’ – and now offshore wind looks investable again
item.description: The investor is shifting across offshore wind, onshore wind, solar and batteries as COO Martin Neubert explains how local presence, being technology agnostic and having auctions that actually work are now decisive to returns

item.title: US offshore wind setback deepens as Ocean Winds takes refund deal
item.description: Joint venture of EDP and Engie in Total-pioneered refund-for-fossil trade on two early-stage projects, but no word on SouthCoast array

item.title: GE Vernova blade break halts wind farm using biggest onshore turbines
item.description: Probe underway after incident involving 6.1MW onshore machine at project built to fulfil Microsoft power deal

item.title: US wind power prices rocket on data centre boom and Trump supply crunch
item.description: New report by LevelTen sees 24% spike in power purchase agreement rates as hyperscalers jockey for capacity in tight market

item.title: RWE Offshore CEO rules out returning projects after UK auction win
item.description: Sven Utermöhlen tells Recharge company not worried about executing large volume of UK projects, which is ‘almost business as usual’

item.title: Nordex shares soar as earnings beat forecasts
item.description: Wind OEM claims strong start to 2026 as demand stays robust in core markets

item.title: How Europe's wind turbine giants pay their CEOs – and what it tells us
item.description: Vestas, Nordex and Siemens Gamesa are raising executive pay as profits recover from a historic margin crisis, but their bonus structures reward different things and create a striking pay gap

item.title: Deals of the Week: TotalEnergies, RWE, Enviromena, Enercon, Galp
item.description: French supermajor chooses Chinese turbines for megaproject and solar projects keep getting bigger

item.title: Siemens Gamesa is creeping closer to its goal, owner reveals
item.description: Wind OEM narrows loss in second quarter as parent group reioterates break-even forecast

item.title: Breakeven in sight? Siemens Gamesa slashes quarterly loss by €200m
item.description: Wind OEM narrows loss in second quarter as parent group reioterates break-even forecast

item.title: Vattenfall UK chief on support shake-up: 'meddling is always dangerous'
item.description: Generator says it will look at UK plan on 'project-by-project basis' as analysts say fine details will make or break investor confidence in scheme

item.title: Ireland promises to prioritise grid package when it takes over EU presidency
item.description: EU grids package is seen as fundamental to unlocking cheaper, cleaner energy

item.title: Ireland makes EU grid package ‘number one priority’ to unlock wind expansion
item.description: EU grids package is seen as fundamental to unlocking cheaper, cleaner energy

item.title: TotalEnergies to build $1.2bn wind farm with Chinese turbines
item.description: Project will link 1GW of onshore wind with major battery storage

item.title: Nato official warns offshore wind industry of Russian and Chinese threat to infrastructure
item.description: Russia is targeting Europe’s energy infrastructure while China is placing malware, high-ranking Nato cyber expert tells WindEurope panel on offshore wind security

item.title: The trust challenge: Why local protests and fake news threaten wind’s €5 trillion expansion
item.description: The Madrid summit showed an extremely aligned industry with a singular core message to politicians. But they can be outflanked by opponents who do not play by the rules

item.title: EU warned over 'disastrous' PPA own goal just as it tries to promote them
item.description: Proposed eco-rules could remove incentive for big players to invest in power deals for wind and solar farms, bloc is warned

item.title: Solar beats wind for first time ever after 'unprecedented' growth
item.description: Chinsese PV expansion helps global output to new highs as renewables edge out coal, says analyst Ember

item.title: Vestas CEO warns of 'real killers and poison' to offshore wind in impassioned remarks
item.description: Chief of Danish wind turbine giant says that ‘we god damn sit here’ and create complexity when every day matters amid Iran energy crisis

item.title: Why BlackRock-backed developer bets on German battery market despite policy uncertainty
item.description: BlackRock-backed Akaysha says Germany energy storage demand outweighs concerns over key grid measures

item.title: GE Vernova books turbine deals in resurgent German wind market
item.description: New York-based energy giant still facing deep losses from its wind segment despite the new orders

item.title: Revenue risk or stability? Why Statkraft and CIP are split on UK’s new wind farm rules
item.description: Good intentions can have unforeseen, negative consequences, warns Statkraft boss – while investor in principle applauds UK government move

item.title: State ends landmark grid deal as offshore wind ambitions collapse
item.description: Former industry leader New Jersey's 7.55GW industry unraveled amid inflation and supply chain headwinds and Trump’s onslaught, but state remains committed to future growth

item.title: War-hit Ukraine bets on wind with massive new project
item.description: Nation's largest private energy firm signals nation remains open for investment amid Russian attacks

item.title: Engie latest to seek US offshore wind exit via Trump refund talks
item.description: French half of Ocean Winds JV with EDP disclosed it was seeking an out from beleaguered SouthCoast and New York Bight arrays hit by President's assault

item.title: Enercon signs large framework deal to supply new flagship turbine to Canada
item.description: German manufacturer to deliver flagship E-175 EP5 E2 turbines with cold climate system and blade heating
